title: parse_sse_block returns a 2-tuple instead of a named struct
description: parse_sse_block returns (SseEvent, Option<String>). The second element is the SSE event id, which is semantically distinct from the event itself. At every call site, callers destructure the tuple or discard the id with _id. A 2-tuple carries no semantic labels; a reader must go to the doc comment to learn what the second element is. Rewrite: define pub struct SseFrame { pub event: SseEvent, pub id: Option<String> } and return SseFrame from parse_sse_block. This makes the pairing explicit, improves call sites (frame.event, frame.id), and eliminates the need for the doc comment to explain the tuple layout.
